data.title: VIQ Solutions announces retirement of COO Susan Sumner
data.description:
• VIQ Solutions (TSX:VQS:CA) on Tuesday announced the retirement of Susan Sumner as chief operating officer (COO) and president of the company.
  • Sumner will continue as a part-time advisor to the Company until January 31, 2025. In connection with her retirement, Sumner has informed the company that she will resign from her director position on the board of directors of the company as of January 31, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allurion Technologies files for $100M mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• Allurion Technologies (NYSE:ALUR) filed for $100M mixed securities shelf.
  • This preliminary prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • Filing

data.title: 1911 Gold upsizes non-brokered private placement to $8.1M
data.description:
• 1911 Gold Corporation (TSXV:AUMB:CA) upsized its previously announced non-brokered private placement offering to now raise aggregate gross proceeds of up to $8.1M.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about December 23, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cellectar stock plunges 50% post-market on restructuring news
data.description:
Cellectar Biosciences (NASDAQ:CLRB) stock plunged 50% post-market after the company announced it plans to lay off around 60% of its workforce as it seeks a partner or buyer for its iopofosine |131 clinical program.

The biotech company said it expects the layoffs to be completed by the end of the year. The restructuring should extend its cash runway into Q3 2025.

Cellectar said it made the decision after determining it would have to run a confirmatory study aimed at generating progression-free survival data in order to seek accelerated approval for iopofonise | 131 for Waldenstrom’s macroglobulinemia.

It added that iopofonise | 131 would be better brought to market by a larger organization with more resources and plans to pursue strategic options for the program, according to a statement.

Cellectar said it plans to focus its resources on advancing its radiopharmaceutical drug candidates CLR-121225 and CLR-121125 into Phase 1 testing for solid tumor cancers. It intends to file Investigational New Drug applications with the FDA for both candidates in the first half of 2025.

data.title: Stitch Fix shares rally as FQ2 loss narrows, outlook beats street view
data.description:
With the discontinuation of its UK business and increased sales per customer, Stitch Fix (NASDAQ:SFIX) realized a narrower loss in the fiscal first quarter, beating Wall Street’s expectations and launching shares more than 22% higher in after-hours trading.

The $0.05 loss per share – versus a loss of $0.22 per share a year ago – was achieved despite a 12.6% drop in sales and 18.6% loss in active clients from a year ago. Net revenue per active client, however, increased 5% year-over-year to $531 and cost of goods sold decreased by 15%.

The improvement towards profitability coupled with a wider profit market encouraged a bullish outlook for the fiscal second quarter and for fiscal 2025.

“Our fiscal year is off to a strong start. We exceeded our expectations in the first quarter on the top and bottom lines,” said CEO Matt Baer. “Our clients are responding to the newness we have brought to our assortment, as well as the improvements we’ve made to our client experience. This progress is a testament to the Stitch Fix team’s ongoing execution of our transformation strategy, and we continue to expect to return to revenue growth by the end of FY26.”

For the fiscal second quarter, Stitch Fix (SFIX) expects revenue to decline by 9% to 12% to be within a range of $290M to $300M, exceeding expectations of $283.6M. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $8M and $13M.

For the fiscal year ending July 31, the company expects revenue to decline by 10%-13% to be within a range of $1.14B and $1.18B. This compares to the consensus estimate of $1.14B. Adjusted EBITDA is targeted for $25M to $36M. Gross margin for both FQ2 and FY25 is to be approximately 44% to 45%.

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 500K barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a surprise build of 499K barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending December 6, after API reported an unexpected 1.23M-barrel build a week earlier.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a build of 2.85M barrels for the week, distillate inventories show a build of 2.45M barrels, and stockpiles at the Cushing, Oklahoma, storage hub show a draw of 1.51M barrels.

The Energy Information Administration will release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report Wednesday morning; analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast domestic commercial crude stocks will decline by 1M barrels, gasoline inventories will increase by 600K barrels, and distillate inventories will increase by 1M barrels.

WTI January crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $68.48/bbl after settling Tuesday at $68.59/bbl.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:USO)

data.title: Satellogic files to sell 3.57M Class A ordinary shares for holders
data.description:
• Satellogic (NASDAQ:SATL) said it has entered into a share purchase agreement, pursuant to which the company issued in a private placement an aggregate of 3.57M Class A ordinary shares to a purchaser at a purchase price of $2.80 per share.
  • The closing of the private placement occurred on December 10 and the company received gross proceeds of $10M.
  • The net proceeds from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes.

  • Filing

data.title: Boeing secures $450M U.S. Air Force contract
data.description:
• Boeing (NYSE:BA) has been awarded a $450.5M contract action by the U.S. Air Force for the F-15 Japan Super Interceptor Program requirement to incorporate C2 production Lot One.
  • The contract provides for the acquisition of radars, self-protection systems, and mission computer units.
  • Work will be performed in St. Louis, Missouri, and is expected to be completed by February 2030.
  • This contract involves Foreign Military Sales (FMS) to Japan. FMS funds in the amount of $110.8M are being obligated at the time of award.
  • The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center, Wright-Patterson Air Force Base, Ohio, is the contracting activity.

data.title: Summit Hotel Properties announces acquisition of two-hotel portfolio for $96M
data.description:
• Summit Hotel Properties (NYSE:INN), through its joint venture with GIC, announced on Tuesday that it is under contract to acquire the 250-guestroom Hampton Inn Boston - Logan Airport and the 149-guestroom Hilton Garden Inn Tysons Corner for a combined purchase price of $96M, or $241,000 per key.
  • The purchase price represents an 8.8% capitalization rate based on forecasted 2024 net operating income and no meaningful capital expenditures are planned before the fourth quarter of 2026.
  • The transaction is expected to be financed with a mix of cash on hand and a $50 million term loan raised with exercising a portion of the accordion option on the GIC joint venture's existing $200 million credit facility.
  • The term loan will have an interest rate of SOFR + 210 basis points, and a fully extended maturity date of September 2028.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Summit Hotel Properties (INN) shares were up 1.16% in post-market trading.

data.title: Chevron completes upgrade at Pasadena refinery to boost capacity, product flexibility
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) said Tuesday it completed an expansion of its Pasadena, Texas, refinery that will allow it to process more light crude oil from the Permian Basin and supply more products to customers on the U.S. Gulf Coast.

The company said the Light Tight Oil project should increase the facility's capacity to process lighter grades of crude oil by nearly 15% to 125K bbl/day, and increase the refinery's product flexibility, allowing it to produce jet fuel and export gas oil.

Chevron (CVX) also expects to realize cost savings by having the Pasadena facility work in tandem with its 375K bbl/day Pascagoula refinery in Mississippi.

Some of the additional gasoline produced at Pasadena may be earmarked for new Chevron (CVX) retail stations in Texas or Louisiana, and the facility also may produce some feedstock for the complex Pascagoula refinery, OPIS reports.

Chevron (CVX) last year produced ~360K bbl/day of crude oil from its Permian Basin operations.
